codeReview.summary: The feature correctly wires webhook intake to create/cancel review_runs atomically with hook_events and covers the open/update/close/merge/replay paths with good tests, but one intended behavior is still unmet: cancelling a running run does not stop downstream processing, so a cancelled run can still finish and be marked completed. I also found a smaller state-tracking issue where close/merge events can erase the stored MR head SHA when GitLab omits last_commit.id.

issues.description: Close/merge events now mark running rows as cancelled (`internal/db/queries/review_runs.sql:70-73`), but the worker path never re-checks that status before continuing. `processClaimedRun` calls `s.processor.ProcessRun(ctx, run)` and then unconditionally marks the same row completed on success, so a run cancelled while already running can still proceed into downstream writeback/comment work and overwrite the cancelled state. That violates the feature's expected behavior that cancelled runs do not continue into comment-writing work.

`cancelRuns` always writes `merge_requests.head_sha = ev.HeadSHA` when closing/merging. `NormalizedEvent` explicitly allows missing `last_commit.id` via `HeadSHADeferred`/empty `HeadSHA` (`internal/hooks/normalize.go:35-43,141-164`), so close/merge payloads without `last_commit` will blank out a previously known MR HEAD SHA instead of preserving it. That weakens later lifecycle state for no benefit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `go-integration` skill should mention that webhook/adapter integration tests may need an external `*_test` package when the adapter under test depends on another package that already imports the current package; otherwise workers can hit an avoidable import-cycle trap while following the prescribed handler-test-first workflow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The feature's handoff sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` and explains the deviation: handler lifecycle coverage had to move into `internal/hooks/handler_lifecycle_integration_test.go` (`package hooks_test`) because importing `internal/runs` from the existing in-package handler tests created an import cycle.

summary: Fail: webhook-triggered run creation/cancellation is mostly implemented well, but running runs are only relabeled in the database and can still complete downstream work, so the lifecycle is not fully enforced end-to-end. Review written from the feature diff, current code, handoff, and transcript skeleton.
